title: SSA State-Agency Fiscal-Year Workload (SSA-SA-FYWL), All States, FY2001–FY2024 (latest-vintage alias)
description: Latest-vintage pointer to the SSA State Agency fiscal-year workload file (SSA-SA-FYWL): per-state, per-federal-fiscal-year adult and SSI-child disability statistics (population denominators, beneficiary counts, receipts, filing rates, favorable/all determinations, allowance rates) for all states and territories. This `-LATEST` alias always reflects the newest published vintage; it currently equals the FY2001–FY2024 vintage.

columns.name: Update Date
columns.description: Date SSA last updated the source file (M/D/YYYY).

columns.name: State Code
columns.description: Two-letter state/territory code (raw values carry a trailing space).

columns.name: Population age 18-64\*
columns.description: Adult (18–64) population denominator. Note: in this LATEST vintage the asterisk in this and other column headers is backslash-escaped (\*).

columns.name: Percent of Adult Population Receiving SSA Adult Disability Benefits
Percent of Population under age 18 Receiving SSI DC Benefits

geographic_coverage: All U.S. states and territories (per-state rows)

source.title: SSA State Agency Fiscal Year Workload Data (SSA-SA-FYWL.csv)

notes: Authoritative: official SSA published workload file. This is the '-LATEST' latest-vintage pointer; it currently equals the dated sibling ssa-sa-fywl-all-states-2001-2024.csv (same FY2001–FY2024 coverage, same 1248 data rows, identical data values). It is NOT byte-identical to that sibling: the difference is confined to the header row only — in this LATEST vintage the header asterisks are backslash-escaped (e.g. 'Population age 18-64\*') and double spaces are collapsed to single, whereas the dated 2001-2024 file uses raw '*' and retains some double spaces. All 1248 data rows are byte-for-byte identical between the two files. Source provenance: ssa-sa-fywl-all-states-2001-LATEST.csv.source.txt (latest FY: 2024).
